project.name: OID Knowledge Lab: Qwen Remediation Autopilot
project.target_user: PKI, SNMP/MIB, IAM, and internal registry owners who need a safe OID inventory review path
project.core_claim: Turn a sanitized OID inventory into an evidence-backed remediation queue with deterministic checks, Qwen-assisted explanation, and human approval gates.

devpost_fields.tagline: Qwen-assisted OID remediation handoffs with deterministic registry checks and human approval gates.
devpost_fields.project_pitch: OID remediation teams often start with messy spreadsheets, unclear private enterprise arcs, and malformed values. This project turns a sanitized OID inventory into a reviewable remediation package: deterministic parsing and registry lookup identify evidence gaps, Qwen summarizes the findings in stakeholder-readable language, and the workflow stops before any external action until a human approves it.
devpost_fields.built_with: Node.js, static HTML/CSS/JavaScript, public IANA PEN data, OID-base sitemap metadata, DashScope OpenAI-compatible Qwen chat API adapter, Markdown/JSON/CSV artifact generation.
devpost_fields.what_it_does: It accepts safe OID inventory input, classifies known enterprise roots and malformed values, generates a remediation queue, drafts reviewer-friendly summaries, and exports public-safe proof artifacts.
devpost_fields.how_qwen_is_used: Qwen is used as the language reasoning layer after deterministic classification. It summarizes evidence, explains ambiguous rows, drafts next-action wording, and preserves human approval gates rather than changing production systems or contacting third parties.
devpost_fields.challenges: The main challenge is keeping the public demo useful without copying third-party page bodies, exposing customer inventories, or overstating live-cloud proof. The implementation separates deterministic evidence from the Qwen reasoning layer and labels the remaining live-run proof gap explicitly.

scenes.title: Problem
scenes.narration: Show a messy but sanitized OID inventory and explain why malformed values and unclear enterprise roots slow remediation.

scenes.title: Deterministic checks
scenes.narration: Run the local assessment to classify valid OIDs, invalid values, public PEN matches, and unresolved items.

scenes.title: Qwen reasoning layer
scenes.narration: Show the Qwen adapter request shape and explain that Qwen drafts stakeholder-readable summaries from deterministic evidence.

scenes.title: Human gate
scenes.narration: Highlight blocked actions: no vendor contact, ticket creation, registry edits, or customer-facing changes without approval.

scenes.title: Generated handoff
scenes.narration: Open the Markdown/CSV/JSON artifacts and show the remediation queue, proof links, and acceptance checks.

scenes.title: Why it matters
scenes.narration: Close with the buyer value: faster OID cleanup scoping for PKI, SNMP/MIB, IAM, and registry owners.

proof_checklist.label: Live Qwen run
proof_checklist.evidence: Requires DASHSCOPE_API_KEY and redacted response receipt

proof_checklist.label: Public demo video
proof_checklist.evidence: Use the three-minute demo script in this pack

public_boundaries: No secrets or API keys in generated artifacts.
No raw customer inventories in public pages.
No private account exports, payment data, identity documents, cookies, or OTPs.
No external action is represented as complete unless a human-approved receipt exists.
